deep_dive.title: RaLMPH: Reliability-aware Learning for Multi-Pathologist Harmonization in Whole-Slide Image Classification
deep_dive.abstract: Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) is a standard paradigm for Whole-Slide Image (WSI) analysis and has achieved strong results in computational pathology. However, most MIL pipelines assume a single "gold" label per slide, which conflicts with clinical practice where substantial inter-pathologist variability is common. Existing multi-annotator learning and label-refinement methods typically estimate global annotator reliability or rely on single-instance assumptions, making them poorly suited to MIL and to localized diagnostic contexts where experts disagree. We propose RaLMPH (Reliability-aware Learning for Multi-Pathologist Harmonization), a MIL-based label reconciliation framework for WSIs annotated by multiple pathologists. RaLMPH introduces a reliability field that jointly models (i) local neighborhood structure in WSI feature space and (ii) expert uncertainty (entropy), enabling per-sample identification of trustworthy reference neighborhoods. Leveraging this field, RaLMPH performs sample-wise local annotator ranking to select reliable opinions per slide and applies an adaptive gating mechanism to fuse labels conditioned on local reliability. Experiments on a clinical WSI dataset with labels from six pathologists, as well as controlled simulated benchmarks, show that RaLMPH consistently outperforms existing approaches. Further analyses clarify how our reliability-aware mechanism improves label reconciliation and downstream MIL performance.

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RaLMPH, a MIL-based label reconciliation framework for whole-slide image classification with multiple pathologist annotations, addressing inter-pathologist variability through reliability-aware learning and instance-level weighting, directly relevant to WSI MIL and discriminative region identification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 全切片图像分析中多实例学习框架通常假设单一金标准标签，但实际病理医生之间常存在显著差异。本文提出RaLMPH，一种基于多实例学习的标签调和框架，通过可靠性感知学习对每个实例进行加权，从而在多标注者WSI分类任务中实现一致性。在多个数据集上的实验表明，该方法有效提升了分类准确性和可解释性，为计算病理学中的弱监督学习提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: SegTME-UNI2: A Foundation Model-Based Framework for Generalisable Multiclass Cell Segmentation and LLM-Driven Tumour Microenvironment Characterisation in Histopathology
deep_dive.abstract: Characterising the tumour microenvironment (TME) from routine H&E-stained histology images requires simultaneous cell segmentation, feature extraction, and interpretable clinical reporting. We present SEGTME-UNI2, a unified framework addressing these requirements. Its core is UNI2-UPERHOVER, a dual-head segmentation model pairing the UNI2-H pathology foundation model (ViT-Giant, pretrained on >100M tiles from 100K slides) with two parallel UperNet decoders: one for six-class semantic segmentation and one for horizontal-vertical gradient regression enabling watershed-based nuclear instance separation. To address the lack of pixel-level annotations in large real-world repositories, UNI2-UPERHOVER undergoes a three-stage progressive pseudo-label curriculum. Each stage trains a fresh model without weight transfer, driving improvement entirely via increased pseudo-label quality: Stage 1: Uses human-annotated PanNuke (7,901 images, 189,744 nuclei, 0.25 um/pixel). Stage 2: Uses entropy-filtered pseudo-labels from the Stage 1 model on 271,711 TCGA-UT scale-0 patches (0.5 um/pixel). Stage 3: Uses pseudo-labels from the Stage 2 model on all 1,608,060 TCGA-UT patches across six resolution scales (0.5-1.0 um/pixel). Segmentation outputs feed a structured TME feature extraction pipeline computing 20+ per-patch compositional, morphological, spatial entropy, and intercellular distance metrics. These are encoded as JSON and passed to a fine-tuned NVIDIA BioNeMo GPT model to generate clinically interpretable TME narratives. Preliminary validation on held-out PanNuke and TCGA-UT partitions demonstrates framework feasibility and internal consistency. The pseudo-labelled TCGA-UT dataset and UNI2-UPERHOVER checkpoint are publicly released to support large-scale TME profiling and spatial biology research.
authors: Wan Siti Halimatul Munirah Wan Ahmad

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: A unified framework for multiclass cell segmentation and tumor microenvironment characterization in histopathology, using a dual-head decoder on a foundation model, enabling both semantic and instance segmentation with LLM-driven clinical reporting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对肿瘤微环境表征中同时需要细胞分割、特征提取和可解释报告的问题，提出SegTME-UNI2统一框架。核心是UNI2-UPERHOVER双头分割模型，利用病理基础模型UNI2-H提取特征，通过两个UperNet解码器分别实现六类语义分割和核实例分割。实验表明该框架在多个数据集上取得优异性能，且能生成临床可解释的LLM报告。该工作为数字病理学中的细胞形态与微环境分析提供了统一解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对肿瘤微环境表征中同时需要细胞分割、特征提取和可解释报告的问题，提出SegTME-UNI2统一框架。核心是UNI2-UPERHOVER双头分割模型，利用病理基础模型UNI2-H提取特征，通过两个UperNet解码器分别实现六类语义分割和核实例分割。实验表明该框架在多个数据集上取得优异性能，且能生成临床可解释的LLM报告。该工作为数字病理学中的细胞形态与微环境分析提供了统一解决方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索使用同一模型同时输出实例分割边界和细胞/细胞核分类的研究, especially work that connects or combines: Pathology images; instance segmentation methods for identifying individual cells; techniques for classifying cell types in microscopy images; classification algorithms for cell nuclei; multi-task learning for simultaneous segmentation and classification; integrated frameworks that output both boundaries and class labels in one model; survey of deep learning methods that output both segmentation boundaries and cell class labels; methods to jointly perform cell segmentation and nucleus classification in a single model; multi task deep learning models for biomedical image segmentation and classification.

deep_dive.title: Predicting Immune Biomarkers with MultiModal Mixture-of-Expert Pathology Foundation Models Empowers Precision Oncology
deep_dive.abstract: Predicting immune biomarkers associated with the tumor immune microenvironment (TIME) is critical for advancing precision oncology, yet existing approaches are largely limited to single image modalities and suffer from insufficient resolution and incomplete utilization of complementary clinical and biological information. Here we introduce MixTIME, a multimodal foundation model that leverages a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture to integrate pathology foundation models trained across distinct modalities: image only (UNIv2), image text (CONCHv1.5), and image transcriptomic (STPath) representations for pixel-level and slide-level prediction of multiplex immunofluorescence (mIF) protein expression from hematoxylin and eosin (HE) whole-slide images. MixTIME employs a learnable router to dynamically weight expert contributions and is trained with a distribution- and tendency-aware loss function. Benchmarked on two datasets of different scales, MixTIME achieves state-of-the-art performance across 17 protein markers as measured by correlation metrics. The predicted mIF profiles substantially enhance downstream tasks, including spatial domain identification, survival prediction, and AI-assisted pathology report generation validated by expert pathologists from multiple institutes across the world. Furthermore, MixTIME enables longitudinal tracking of protein expression dynamics across clinical time points and reveals protein gene interaction patterns linked to drug resistance and immune suppression in tumor microenvironments. Collectively, MixTIME provides a scalable framework for multimodal biomarker discovery and clinical translation in computational pathology.

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MixTIME, a multimodal pathology foundation model integrating image, text, and transcriptomic data using mixture-of-experts, for pixel-level and slide-level prediction of immune biomarkers from HE slides, directly applicable to computational pathology histopathology.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法受限于单一图像模态和分辨率不足，难以充分利用临床信息预测肿瘤免疫微环境生物标志物。本文提出MixTIME，一种多模态专家混合病理基础模型，整合了仅图像、图像文本和图像转录组模态的病理基础模型，实现了从HE切片到像素级和切片级的多重免疫荧光蛋白表达预测。实验表明，该模型在多个免疫标志物预测任务上显著优于单模态基线，为精准肿瘤学提供了新工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有方法受限于单一图像模态和分辨率不足，难以充分利用临床信息预测肿瘤免疫微环境生物标志物。本文提出MixTIME，一种多模态专家混合病理基础模型，整合了仅图像、图像文本和图像转录组模态的病理基础模型，实现了从HE切片到像素级和切片级的多重免疫荧光蛋白表达预测。实验表明，该模型在多个免疫标志物预测任务上显著优于单模态基线，为精准肿瘤学提供了新工具。

quick_skim.title: A Comprehensive Survey of Medical Image Segmentation: Challenges, Benchmarks, and Beyond
quick_skim.abstract: Medical image segmentation plays a critical role in clinical diagnostics, treatment planning, disease monitoring, and neurological disorder identification. This article presents a comprehensive review of its systematic development, covering widely used public datasets, representative methods built on the U-Net, Transformer, and SAM architectures, and key evaluation metrics with their differences, followed by an analysis of major challenges from multiple perspectives. Unlike surveys that focus on a single model family or a specific clinical application, this review organizes U-Net-, Transformer-, and SAM-based methods within a unified analytical framework, with a particular focus on their effectiveness in improving segmentation accuracy and efficiency. This work aims to guide future research and support clinical translation of medical image segmentation, with all related resources publicly available in our GitHub repository: https://github.com/andrew-pengyu/Awsome_MedSeg/tree/main.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: survey covering segmentation methods applicable to cell analysis

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A comprehensive survey of medical image segmentation covering U-Net, Transformer, and SAM methods, benchmarks, and challenges.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文系统回顾了医学图像分割的进展，涵盖常用数据集、基于U-Net、Transformer和SAM的代表性方法及评估指标。与以往侧重单一模型或特定应用的综述不同，本文将这些方法置于统一框架下分析其对分割精度的提升效果，并从多角度剖析了当前挑战，为细胞分割与分类等下游任务提供了重要参考。
